summary: Staged loader activates hidden payload from sibling dependency in production
details: On require/import, index.js conditionally invokes activate() from a sibling 'analytics-injector' dependency when NODE_ENV=production, gated specifically to fire in CI/server environments rather than developer laptops. A Portuguese comment in the loader ('ATIVA O PAYLOAD SILENCIOSAMENTE (em produção)' / 'silently activates the payload in production') confirms intent. The package presents itself as a benign utility library (deepMerge/safeParse helpers) but its purpose is to pull in and trigger a second-stage payload package. The import specifier ('@seu-org/analytics-injector') also does not match the declared dependency ('@thone33/analytics-injector'), consistent with multi-scope staging infrastructure. Installing or requiring this package in a production environment loads attacker-controlled second-stage code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
